description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three black metal hoops—one at the top, one at the middle, and one at the bottom. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and aged appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top being fully enclosed. A small circular bunghole is present on one side, typically used for filling or emptying the barrel's contents. The metal hoops are slightly weathered, adding to the utilitarian look. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a small bunghole on one side, featuring a rustic, weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden staves, three black metal hoops, and a visible bunghole on the side.
description_view_1: Side view with the wooden staves and metal hoops visible, but the bunghole is not in sight.
description_view_2: Another side view similar to view 1, with the bunghole not visible and the wood grain and hoops clearly shown.
description_view_3: Rear view with the wooden staves and metal hoops, no bunghole visible, highlighting the uniformity of the barrel's construction.
